questions.question: Oxides of nitrogen are given out from car exhausts. Which row best shows why oxides of nitrogen are unwanted in the atmosphere? acidic toxic A no no, B no yes, C yes no, D yes yes

questions.question: Water is purified using several processes. Four of the processes are listed. 1   Chlorine is added to water to kill any bacteria. 2   Water is passed through coarse gravel to remove large pieces of dirt. 3   Water is passed through wire screens to remove large twigs. 4   Water is passed through fine sand to remove small particles. In which order are the processes carried out? A 1 ď‚® 2 ď‚® 3 ď‚® 4, B 2 ď‚® 1 ď‚® 4 ď‚® 3, C 3 ď‚® 2 ď‚® 4 ď‚® 1, D 4 ď‚® 3 ď‚® 2 ď‚® 1
questions.text: Water is purified using several processes. Four of the processes are listed. 1   Chlorine is added to water to kill any bacteria. 2   Water is passed through coarse gravel to remove large pieces of dirt. 3   Water is passed through wire screens to remove large twigs. 4   Water is passed through fine sand to remove small particles. In which order are the processes carried out? A 1 ď‚® 2 ď‚® 3 ď‚® 4, B 2 ď‚® 1 ď‚® 4 ď‚® 3, C 3 ď‚® 2 ď‚® 4 ď‚® 1, D 4 ď‚® 3 ď‚® 2 ď‚® 1

questions.question: When solid S is heated strongly, it forms gas G. G turns limewater cloudy. What are S and G and which type of reaction does S undergo? S G type of reaction A calcium carbonate carbon dioxide combustion, B calcium carbonate carbon dioxide thermal decomposition, C sodium carbonate oxygen combustion, D sodium carbonate oxygen thermal decomposition 12
questions.text: When solid S is heated strongly, it forms gas G. G turns limewater cloudy. What are S and G and which type of reaction does S undergo? S G type of reaction A calcium carbonate carbon dioxide combustion, B calcium carbonate carbon dioxide thermal decomposition, C sodium carbonate oxygen combustion, D sodium carbonate oxygen thermal decomposition 12
options: A calcium carbonate carbon dioxide combustion
B calcium carbonate carbon dioxide thermal decomposition
D sodium carbonate oxygen thermal decomposition 12

questions.question: Petroleum is separated into fractions by fractional distillation. Separation occurs in a fractionating column. Some properties of three of these fractions are shown. fraction boiling point range / ď‚°C number of carbon atoms in the molecules 1  5 â€“10 2 320â€“350 16â€“24 3 120â€“210 Which statement is correct? A Fraction 1 has a higher boiling point range than fraction 2., B Fraction 2 is removed from a higher point in the fractionating column than fraction 1., C Molecules in fraction 3 have shorter chains than those in fraction 2., D None of the fractions are liquid at room temperature. 13
questions.text: Petroleum is separated into fractions by fractional distillation. Separation occurs in a fractionating column. Some properties of three of these fractions are shown. fraction boiling point range / ď‚°C number of carbon atoms in the molecules 1  5 â€“10 2 320â€“350 16â€“24 3 120â€“210 Which statement is correct? A Fraction 1 has a higher boiling point range than fraction 2., B Fraction 2 is removed from a higher point in the fractionating column than fraction 1., C Molecules in fraction 3 have shorter chains than those in fraction 2., D None of the fractions are liquid at room temperature. 13
options: A Fraction 1 has a higher boiling point range than fraction 2.
B Fraction 2 is removed from a higher point in the fractionating column than fraction 1.
C Molecules in fraction 3 have shorter chains than those in fraction 2.
D None of the fractions are liquid at room temperature. 13

questions.question: Which statement describes methane? A It is an alcohol., B It is an unsaturated molecule., C It contains carbon, hydrogen and oxygen atoms only., D Each molecule contains four single covalent bonds.
questions.text: Which statement describes methane? A It is an alcohol., B It is an unsaturated molecule., C It contains carbon, hydrogen and oxygen atoms only., D Each molecule contains four single covalent bonds.
options: B It is an unsaturated molecule.
C It contains carbon, hydrogen and oxygen atoms only.
D Each molecule contains four single covalent bonds.

questions.question: The structure of propane is shown. HC H H C H H C H H H Which statement about the atoms and the bonding in propane is correct? A All the bonds are single bonds., B Each carbon atom only bonds with two hydrogen atoms., C Propane is an unsaturated molecule., D There are three carbon-carbon bonds. 14
questions.text: The structure of propane is shown. HC H H C H H C H H H Which statement about the atoms and the bonding in propane is correct? A All the bonds are single bonds., B Each carbon atom only bonds with two hydrogen atoms., C Propane is an unsaturated molecule., D There are three carbon-carbon bonds. 14
options: A All the bonds are single bonds.
B Each carbon atom only bonds with two hydrogen atoms.
C Propane is an unsaturated molecule.
D There are three carbon-carbon bonds. 14
